channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Streetlight disturbing sleep, 19th June
item.description:
There's excessive light pollution overspilling into my bedrooms disturbing sleep sat 10 County Grove. Please either fit a cowl to the streetlight outside the property or cover the cost of blackout blinds (probably the cheapest option).Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): County Grove, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cut tree branches overhanging onto footpath, 19th June
item.description:
Tripping hazard - cut logs have been left in situ but overhang onto footpath so people could fall over them or bruise their legs. Especially dangerous at night when path isn't well lit.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glassmill Lane, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Mattress dumped on street, 18th June
item.description:
Mattress dumped on corner of Whiteorth Road and a Herbert Road again

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Herbert Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Noisy works being done at 11pm, 18th June
item.description:
Why are council men making noise at 11pm clanging metal, opening drains or manholes, using noisy machinery, at this time? It wouldn't be ok if it were a member of the public so why is it on to be doing noisy works as the council? If not the council then a utility. Not acceptable.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Heathfield Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Utility Works and Temporary Traffic Lights

item.description:
Piles of rubbish outside convenience store

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Herbert Road, Woolwich

item.title: Flytipped bin by bus stop C, 18th June
item.description:
Someone dumped their old rubbish bin on the street, and people have started to shove rubbish into it but it’s overflowing. This could be because the council removed the bin from the bus stop some time ago.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 129, Gipsy Hill, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Paget Rise, Plumstead

item.title: Unsuitable use of recycle bags to dump rubbish on the floor, 18th June
item.description:
Flat 2,is continuously dumping household and garden waste outside the front of the house on the street. This has caused rubbish bags to pile up, creating an unsightly and unhygienic environment and attracting vermin. This ongoing issue requires urgent act

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Blurton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 5 NOX Canisters dumped on pavement, 18th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 100, College Road, Dulwich, Greater London

item.title: Abandoned untaxed vehicle with unsafe damage to near side front wheel, 18th June
item.description:
The car is untaxed and without a valid permit as it received a parking ticket today. The car has severe damage to the front near side wheel making it undrivable and unsafe. The car is parked outside 30/32 harcourt Road and been there a few days

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harcourt Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Used serringe and needle, 18th June
item.description:
A serringe and a needle are on the pavement outside 92 angell road. No picture available. Please organise safe collection.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Angell Road, Loughborough Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: They take up five spaces as well as the one that’s theirs., 18th June
item.description:
It leaves disabled people with no where to park close then there home. It causes sever damage to cars when they fall. Which they do a lot. The noise caused by the man who throws them about at 4:00am is completely unacceptable. Something needs to be done about this. It’s really a terrible situation.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Colville Square, Notting Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Priory Park Road, London

item.title: By Kilburn Library, 18th June

item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: HGV’s ignoring road signs, 18th June
item.description:
An HGV has blocked Pembroke Road for nearly an hour. Cars have been speeding down the footpaths to get around it. Asking them why they came down here, they said their Sat Nav directed them down here and that they didn’t see the road signs that they ended up moving out of the road so that they could manoeuvre out. Due to so many foreign drivers we need a sign with a picture, not a silly little one on a post 8ft into the road or a floor standing written sign that many drivers cannot read

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pembroke Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I reported the below on the 27th November 2025 report/8474451, 18th June
item.description:
I reported the below on the 27th November 2025 and pass this location most weeks and don't believe it has be resolved .....Beckenham War Memorial roundabout ie junction of Beckenham Road, Rectory Road, High Street and Croydon Road. At Beckenham Road junction Zebra Crossing Belisha Beacon not working tonight. https://fix.bromley.gov.uk/report/8474451
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 251, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
